mnemonics.title: Network Performance Metrics
mnemonics.content: RAPP Time: Response time, Availability, Performance, Perceptual quality - remember 'RAP' stands for Response time, Availability, Performance metrics for network evaluation

mnemonics.title: Transmission Modes
mnemonics.content: SHE - Simplex (Half/Single direction), Half-Duplex (Either way but not together), Full-Duplex (Everything happens together) - remember 'SHE talks but Full can do both'

mnemonics.title: Network Types by Scale
mnemonics.content: PAN MAN LAN WAN - Personal Metropolitan Local Wide - remember as 'People Make Life Wonderful' (PMNW) or in order of increasing size: PAN→LAN→MAN→WAN

acronyms.usage: Building or campus network, high speed, privately owned - like office WiFi
Spans cities/countries, provided by ISPs - like the Internet
City-wide network connecting multiple LANs - like city cable TV network
Smallest network, personal devices within reach - like Bluetooth connection phone to earbuds
Unique hardware address of network interface card at Data Link Layer
Hardware component that connects device to network

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a bicycle wheel with hub in center and spokes radiating to each node - central hub is the critical point, all communication flows through it

visualTips.visualize: Imagine a fishing net or spider web - multiple interconnections provide redundancy, every node connects to multiple others
Think of postal mail: propagation = travel time on roads, transmission = time to write/pack letter, processing = sorting at post office, queuing = waiting in line at post office
Bandwidth = width of highway (number of lanes), Throughput = actual cars passing per second (limited by traffic, accidents, construction)

analogies.analogy: Bandwidth is like pipe diameter (how much water flows), latency is like pipe length (how long water takes to reach end). Wide short pipe = high bandwidth low latency, narrow long pipe = low bandwidth high latency.
Hub is like a party speaker - everyone hears everything. Switch is like a telephone switchboard - calls connect only between intended parties.
Client-Server is like a restaurant where you order from and get food served by waiters. P2P is like a potluck dinner where everyone brings and shares food directly.
Bus = single highway road, Ring = circular jogging track, Star = hub airport with flights to all destinations, Mesh = interconnected highway grid system with many route options.
